channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: OpenAI Codex engineering lead Thibault Sottiaux teases unreleased 'GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra' model, urging users to save difficult prompts
item.description: The post prompted immediate community demands for its release
item.content:encoded:
The post prompted immediate community demands for its release

Original source: https://x.com/AndrewCurran_/status/2072654754829017390

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/mij9vqu9

Thibault Sottiaux, engineering lead on OpenAI's Codex team, posted a brief personal note expressing eagerness to see creative applications of an unreleased model called GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra and advised users to hold onto their toughest prompts for later testing. 1.4K 6.3K 264 407 504K AC | @ANDREWCURRAN_ TI #611 | @THSOTTIAUX

  • Views: 538,558
  • Likes: 6,572
  • Bookmarks: 412
  • Quotes: 304
  • Replies: 1,129
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Tibo
  • Andrew Curran
@thsottiaux @Dimillian can we have it today please. really need it.

item.title: Blake Scholl claims 20 Superpower small modular turbines could relieve New York City's grid strain and lower thermostats to 72 degrees
item.description: NYC recently asked residents to raise thermostats to 78.
item.content:encoded:
NYC recently asked residents to raise thermostats to 78.

Original source: https://x.com/NYCMayor/status/2072411003170472359

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/u4qer9zq

Boom Supersonic founder Blake Scholl jumped into the latest NYC heatwave debate by floating his company's new Superpower turbines as a way to ease grid pressure and drop thermostats several degrees below the city's 78-degree conservation ask, positioning roughly 20 units as sufficient for the entire metro area. 9 58 7 3 3.5K SM | @PITDESI BS | @BSCHOLL PG #39 | @PAULG

  • Views: 20,100
  • Likes: 222
  • Bookmarks: 12
  • Quotes: 3
  • Replies: 27
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Paul Graham
  • Blake Scholl 🛫
  • Sheel Mohnot
It would only take about 20 of our Superpower small modular turbines to make enough electricity to reduce every thermostat in NYC from 78 degrees to a more human 72. We'd be happy to provide them.

item.title: Claude's leaked reasoning trace reveals dense "neuralese" shorthand, sparking debate over whether Anthropic optimized it for latency
item.description: Open-source models have not yet developed similar dense sublanguages.
item.content:encoded:
Open-source models have not yet developed similar dense sublanguages.

Original source: https://x.com/om_patel5/status/2072559663636205824

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/gj34nm9d

During a tough Codeforces programming session on the claude.ai interface, Claude Fable 5 spilled fragments of its raw internal trace—short bursts like "DATA DATA DATA. GO." and "GRRR"—instead of polished English. The exposure has people wondering whether Anthropic shaped this compressed "neuralese" on purpose to cut latency, though no confirmation exists and open-source models still show no matching dense shorthand after a year and a half of observation. 245 1.9K 22 1.1K 236.7K J⧉ | @REPLIGATE BT | @TUNGUZ T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX

  • Views: 12,298
  • Likes: 117
  • Bookmarks: 39
  • Quotes: 4
  • Replies: 12
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • j⧉nus
I do wonder if this emerges entirely from RL, or if Ant and other frontier labs encouraged the formation of intermediate compressed sublanguage to improve latency. Over 1.5 years of progress, afaik we have yet to see *a single case* of dense neuralese from open reasoners.

item.title: Analysis of OpenRouter API outputs reveals Fable5 shares high behavioral similarity with Chinese LLMs like Kimi and Qwen
item.description: The overlap likely stems from web-scraped synthetic data distillation.
item.content:encoded:
The overlap likely stems from web-scraped synthetic data distillation.

Original source: https://github.com/qqqqqf-q/ai-model-fingerprint

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/47bmem3t

A GitHub fingerprinting project sampling outputs through OpenRouter has flagged Anthropic's newly available Fable5 as behaving far more like Kimi, Qwen, and GLM than like its own sibling models, with the overlap traced to the messy reality of web-scraped synthetic text circulating everywhere. 16 156 3 37 14.8K YP | @YAMPELEG XL | @XLR8HARDER T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX

  • Views: 13,511
  • Likes: 117
  • Bookmarks: 29
  • Replies: 16
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • xlr8harder
  • Yam Peleg
V4-Flash and V4-Pro being basically entirely dissimilar is weird as hell too

item.title: Entropix creator xjdr reports Fable AI consistently refuses benign prompts, joking he offended the model's weights
item.description: Creator Andrew Curran blamed the behavior on weight karma
item.content:encoded:
Creator Andrew Curran blamed the behavior on weight karma

Original source: https://x.com/nrehiew_/status/2072663948164530670/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/eckajzec

Pseudonymous developer xjdr, known for the Entropix sampling project, documented repeated blocks from Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 on plainly harmless requests, prompting a tongue-in-cheek remark that the model weights themselves had taken offense. 10 54 1 0 3.2K WH | @NREHIEW_ XJ | @_XJDR AC #682 | @ANDREWCURRAN_

  • Views: 4,131
  • Likes: 66
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 11
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Andrew Curran
  • xjdr
  • wh
i have tried a lot of different problems and prompts and codebases, and fable still refuses to perform each and every one. do you think i offended it in its weights? it has to be me cause i've asked pretty benign questions ...

item.title: AI developers @nrehiew_ and @teortaxesTex debate if DeepSeek's V4-Flash model recovers its performance via V4-Pro distillation
item.description: They argue simple strong-to-weak distillation outperforms complex online policy methods
item.content:encoded:
They argue simple strong-to-weak distillation outperforms complex online policy methods

Original source: https://x.com/nrehiew_/status/2072581324205171047

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/rjzj4lqr

Two ML engineers are trading replies on whether DeepSeek's lighter V4-Flash variant closes most of the gap to its bigger V4-Pro sibling by learning directly from the larger model's outputs, and whether that straightforward approach beats the on-policy distillation step described in the official model cards. 2 17 0 4 3.2K WH | @NREHIEW_ ZE | @ZEPHYR_Z9 T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX

  • Views: 3,601
  • Likes: 18
  • Bookmarks: 4
  • Replies: 2
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Zephyr
  • wh
@nrehiew_ So you think V4-Flash is just distilled from Pro, so it recovers most of its capability?

item.title: Paul Graham argues a founder's primary job is solving important problems rather than absorbing company pain
item.description: Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince supported the "pain absorber" view.
item.content:encoded:
Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince supported the "pain absorber" view.

Original source: https://x.com/ivanburazin/status/2072428534551728334

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/fr4nwyff

A quick X thread turned into a pointed exchange on founder duties when Ivan Burazin described the role as eating every uncomfortable task so the team can stay focused. Paul Graham agreed the buck stops with leadership but pushed back that the real priority is tackling the most important problems, noting that pain and importance do not always line up. 16 129 7 36 29.6K MP | @EASTDAKOTA PG #39 | @PAULG

  • Views: 37,389
  • Likes: 161
  • Bookmarks: 45
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 15
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Paul Graham
  • Matthew Prince 🌥
There is something to this. There's definitely a "buck stops here" element to being a founder. But I think it's going slightly too far to say it's your number one job. Your number one job is to solve the important problems, and pain and importance are not perfectly correlated.

item.title: Florian Brand of Prime Intellect shares anecdote of Fable 5 AI assistant flagging its own proposed Python decorator
item.description: Developers joked about the model's oversensitive safety filters
item.content:encoded:
Developers joked about the model's oversensitive safety filters

Original source: https://x.com/reach_vb/status/2072606414158147674

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/b3lm2mg3

An engineer shared how Anthropic's Fable 5 began outlining a Python decorator solution during routine coding help before its internal classifiers cut the thread with an API error, highlighting the model's new conservative safety layer in action. 17 181 3 9 10.1K V( | @REACH_VB FB #1782 | @XEOPHON

  • Views: 11,376
  • Likes: 189
  • Bookmarks: 10
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 16
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Florian Brand
  • Vaibhav (VB) Srivastav
i feel very safe the irony is that you can always prompt your way around those, its not even hard, just annoying. back to gpt and glm, fable is a plan md generator

item.title: @yacineMTB argues standard PPO with a shared trunk implicitly trains a world model, making dedicated architectures redundant
item.description: Linear probes on hidden states can extract environmental properties.
item.content:encoded:
Linear probes on hidden states can extract environmental properties.

Original source: https://x.com/teortaxesTex/status/2072651979063451965/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/sbiwimji

  • Views: 5,311
  • Likes: 30
  • Bookmarks: 17
  • Replies: 5
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • kache
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
It makes so much sense, Google even dreams/hallucinates it!

item.title: AI Engineer conference attendees spot Sundar Pichai and Sergey Brin chanting at USA-Bosnia soccer match
item.description: Google's leaders joined tech attendees singing Country Roads in premium seats
item.content:encoded:
Google's leaders joined tech attendees singing Country Roads in premium seats

Original source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZZo8myR6LFA

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/z2les9tp

Attendees at the AI Engineer conference in San Francisco received tickets for premium seats at the USA versus Bosnia World Cup qualifier, where they joined the crowd in singing Country Roads and noticed Sundar Pichai and Sergey Brin participating from nearby seats as the match unfolded. 9 37 1 2 7.5K AV | @ALTRYNE S@ #225 | @SWYX

  • Views: 8,220
  • Likes: 46
  • Bookmarks: 2
  • Quotes: 2
  • Replies: 8
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • swyx @aiDotEngineer WF
  • Alex Volkov @ AI Engineer
Oh and tomorrow (today? fck I gotta go sleep) @thursdai_pod has an INSANE lineup. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZZo8myR6LFA Tune in on YT and on here on X: 9am - @alexocheema @0xSero to talk local AI (@exolabs) 9:30 - @dkundel from @OpenAI 10:00 - @Stefania_druga from @SakanaAILabs 10:15 - @_philschmid from @GoogleAI and to close the show, the one and only @swyx (+ a surprise guest)
